Built originally as a private road for King Fahd (SAU), the stretch of Highway 10 connecting Highway 75 in the Haradh area to Highway 95 in the west of Saudi Arabia is 240 km (149 mi) long.
It cuts through the desert with no bends left or right, or any appreciable gradient up or down.
The super-straight stretch has an estimated driving time of around 2 hr.

hits.title: Saudi Arabia's Highway 10 holds the Guinness World Record as the ...
hits.summary:
Saudi Arabia’s Highway 10 holds the Guinness World Record as the longest straight road on Earth, stretching an incredible 240 kilometers (about 149 miles) without a single bend or curve.
This engineering marvel cuts directly through the Arabian desert, offering travelers a surreal view of endless sand dunes on both sides.
...
Rather do the second longest 90 mile straight on the Eyre Highway in Australia, which is a straight, 146.6 km (91.1 mi) stretch of road between Balladonia and Caiguna.

hits.title: This 256 km highway in Saudi Arabia is world's longest road without ...
hits.summary:
# This 256 km highway in Saudi Arabia is world’s longest road without a bend
## Originally constructed as a private road for King Fahd, Highway 10 runs from Haradh, a city renowned for its oil and gas reserves, to Al Batha, near the United Arab Emirates border.
Saudi Arabia’s Highway 10 has emerged as the world’s longest straight road, surpassing Australia’s Eyre Highway, according to the Guinness World Records.
...
This 256-kilometre (159-mile) asphalt stretch cuts through the vast Rub Al-Khali desert, also known as the Empty Quarter, the largest sand desert in the world, without any bends left or right, or any appreciable gradient up or down.
...
Previously, the Eyre Highway, a 146-kilometre-long stretch connecting Western Australia and South Australia, held the record for the straightest road.

hits.title: Highway 10 (Saudi Arabia) - Wikipedia
hits.summary:
|Length|1,480 km (920 mi)|
...
**Highway 10** is a highway in Saudi Arabia, stretching from Ad Darb in Al Darb Governorate to the Saudi–UAE border near the Persian Gulf.
The highway is 1,480 kilometers (920 mi) long.
The highway became famous for its straight section that is 240 kilometers (150 mi) long between Haradh and Al Batha, and has no bends or noticeable gradients, earning it the world record for the longest straight road.
...
The 240-kilometer (150 mi) straight section of Saudi Arabia's Highway 10 has emerged as the world's longest straight road, surpassing the 146-kilometer (91 mi) straight section of Australia's Eyre Highway.

hits.title: World's longest straight road stretches for 150 miles without a single ...
hits.summary:
The longest unbroken stretch of road in the world is located in Saudi Arabia, allowing drivers to navigate for over 150 miles without needing to adjust their steering wheel.
...
This remarkable roadway spans approximately 150 miles without any curves.
It traverses the expansive Rub Al-Khali desert in Saudi Arabia.
...
Highway 10, which connects Haradh to Al Batha, is one of the most extraordinary routes available to everyday drivers.
Originally built for King Fahd, this road runs straight the heart the Rub-Kh desert and has earned the Guinness World Record for being the longest uninterrupted road segment on the planet.
Motorists can travel approximately 240 kilometers (149 miles) without needing to turn the wheel at all.
Previously, Australia’s Eyre Highway held the record, measuring around 146 kilometers (91.25 miles) without a turn.
As reported by Guinness World Records, despite its remarkable length, the journey may not take as long as one would think, according to the Express.
They stated: “Constructed initially as a private route for King Fahd (SAU), the section of Highway 10 linking Highway 75 in the Haradh region to Highway 95 in western Arabia is240 km149 mi long.
It crosses the desert without any bends or significant gradients.
The straight stretch is estimated to take about 2 hours to drive.”

Saudi Arabia’s Highway 10 stretches 1,480 km (920 mi), famed for its 240 km (150 mi) straight section between Haradh and Al Batha with no bends or gradients holding the Guinness World Record for the longest straight road.
Originally a private road for King Fahd across the Rub’ al-Khali desert, it boosts tourism and connectivity in the Eastern Province.

The Stuart Highway ("The Track") is a 2,834 km sealed lifeline connecting Darwin (NT) to Port Augusta.

hits.title: The world's longest straight road is 159-mile mega-motorway ...
hits.summary:
Highway 10, initially constructed as a private thoroughfare for King Fahd, has now officially earned the Guinness World Record for the longest uninterrupted road globally, extending an impressive 159 miles (256 kilometers) without any curves.
This roadway slices through the Rub al Khali, also known as the "Empty Quarter," which is the largest uninterrupted sand desert on Earth.
...
For more than two hours, drivers encounter an unchanging horizon with no hills, no curves, and no variation in the landscape.
Previously, Australia's Eyre Highway held the record with its 91-mile stretch known as the "90 Mile Straight," but Highway 10 has surpassed that mark by nearly 70 miles.

hits.title: The world's straightest road crosses 240 kilometers of desert without ...
hits.summary:
Saudi Arabia’s Highway 10 holds one of the strangest records in modern transport.
A 240 kilometer stretch between Haradh and Al Batha is recognized by Guinness World Records as the longest straight road on the planet, turning a drive through the Empty Quarter into a two-hour lesson in engineering, logistics, and endurance.
...
Highway 10 also surpassed Australia’s Eyre Highway, whose longest straight section measures 146 kilometers.
